doc_title: ADM 03-00-008 OSHA Clearance Process for Non-Policy and Policy Issuances

steps.text: Complete items 1 through 8 on the OSHA 201.
Determine whether field coordination is necessary.
Prepare an electronic version of the OSHA 201 and a draft directive to provide electronically.
Identify all clearance officials and proxies who will receive a copy.
Review, approve, and sign the OSHA 201 before distribution to the clearance officials.
Obtain the approval of their Program Director and sends copies to the clearance officials and proxies.
Review the draft directive.
Complete item number 9 and 10 of the OSHA 201.
Return the OSHA 201 with comments to the originator.
Review and reconcile any comments noted in item 8 of the OSHA 201.
Contact nonresponsive clearance offices prior to the review deadline.
Refer to the Program Director any unresolved disagreements.
Elevate disputes with clearance officials to the appropriate Deputy Assistant Secretary for resolution.
Revise the draft directive and if necessary, complete an additional clearance round for concurrence.
Provide to the Office of the Solicitor, prior to submission to the directives officer, the draft directive for review/concurrence.
Prepare directive clearance packet for processing through the directives officer.
Review the directive clearance packet to determine if complete and accurate.
Provide revisions request from the Office of the Assistant Secretary (OAS) to the originator.
Provide notification of signed directive to the originator and requests the directive be formatted to a 508-compliant PDF file.
Provide the final unsigned 508-compliant PDF file to the directives officer for posting.
Provide the final 508-compliant PDF file directive to the web administrator.
Review 508-compliant PDF file for web-related technical content.
Publish 508-compliant PDF file to OSHA Directives webpage.
Notify the directives officer of availability on the OSHA Directives webpage.
Notify affected parties when the approved directive is posted.
Prepare a cancellation notice using the cancellation template.
Obtain the approval of their Program Director for cancellation.
Provide the cancellation notice and concurrence grid.
Review the cancellation packet to determine if complete and accurate.
Provide revision request from the OAS to the originator for cancellation.
Provide notification of signed cancellation directive to the originator and request the cancellation notice be formatted to 508-compliant PDF.
Provide the final unsigned 508-compliant PDF file of the cancellation to the directives officer.
Provide the final 508-compliant PDF file of the cancellation to the web administrator.
Review 508-compliant PDF of cancellation for web-related technical content.
Publish 508-compliant PDF of cancellation to OSHA Directives webpage.
Notify the directives officer of cancellation availability on the OSHA Directives webpage.
Each year, program offices will conduct a full assessment of the files for the directives approved that year.
Once the assessment is completed, the program head or designee will send an email or memo to the Director, Office of Audit, DAP, or designee.
The program office should retain completion documentation in the directive file record.

preconditions.text: Before a direction may be drafted, the originator will convene a meeting with directorates and regional offices.
Before a direction may go into effect, the originator must provide a copy of the proposed direction to each clearance office.
Prior to clearance review confirm the current directive template is being used.
All final unsigned directives will be 508 compliant before submission by the originating office to the directives officer.

constraints.text: Allow a minimum of 15 working days for review and clearance.
Directions are a minimum of 5 working days for review and clearance.
Directorates MUST NOT provide directives directly to the web administrator.
Regional Offices MUST NOT provide directives directly to the web administrator.
